mappings.rationale: Directly covers genes, chromosomes, the genetic code, gene mutations, gene expression, and regulation—core topics of heredity at the molecular level.
The primary VSI for this section, covering Mendelian genetics, the physical basis of heredity, and the combined action of heredity and environment.
Covers the chemical and molecular nature of genes, the genetic code, applications of molecular genetics, and population-level genomics.
Addresses molecular genetics including DNA structure, the genetic code, gene mutations, and the expression and regulation of genes.
Covers population genetics, gene frequencies, the Hardy-Weinberg principle, and natural selection as an agent of evolutionary change.
Addresses natural selection as an agent of evolutionary change and how sexual selection shapes gene frequencies in populations.
Directly covers attempts to change genetic endowments through deliberate selective measures (eugenics) as outlined in section C.3.
Covers the historical development of understanding heredity and evolution, including Mendel's contribution and natural selection theory.
Examines conceptual foundations of genetics and heredity including the gene concept, population genetics, and the interplay of heredity and environment.
Covers chromosomes as the cellular basis of heredity, including mitosis and meiosis as mechanisms of genetic transmission.
Addresses applications of molecular genetics including genetic engineering, relevant to artificial selection for genetic improvements.
Covers early experimental approaches to understanding the combined action of heredity and environment in producing organisms.
